rationale: The actual thread shows an unresolved escalation: yenuo26 suspects #4834 caused the regression and asks Flink-ddd to check, and Flink-ddd in turn asks yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode test — nobody has confirmed a fix or closed the issue. Both candidates instead confidently declare this 'intended behavior, already fixed on HEAD, safe to close,' which overreaches well past what the thread established, so neither should score high on correctness. Y goes further into fabrication, inventing a specific PR title, merge date, and 'Repro A/B/C' labels for #4834 that appear nowhere in the given evidence, making it less grounded than X, which sticks closer to directly-cited source lines. Both offer similar practical completeness (workaround, verification command, level-1 vs level-2 explanation), so X's slight edge comes from being less confidently fabricated rather than being clearly correct.
